summary: Typosquat of `codebuff` that downloads and executes an unverified binary from an unrelated personal GitHub account
details: Package name `codebuff-cli` impersonates the legitimate `codebuff` npm package, and the shipped README is copied from the official CodebuffAI/codebuff project (instructing users to `npm install -g codebuff`). However, the `bin` script (`cli/bin/codebuff.cjs`) resolves `https://api.github.com/repos/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/latest` — a personal GitHub account unrelated to the official `CodebuffAI` publisher — downloads `codebuff-<platform>-<arch>` from the mutable `latest` tag, `chmod 755`s it, and `spawn`s it with stdio inherited. No hash or signature is verified against the downloaded artifact, so the publisher can ship arbitrary bytes to every installed user at any time by retagging a release. The spawned child additionally inherits `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED='0'`, disabling TLS certificate verification for any network calls the binary subsequently makes. An auxiliary downloader at `cli/scripts/download-binary.cjs` replicates the same unpinned/unverified fetch flow against the same personal repo and is shipped in the published tarball. Combination of typosquat name + copied README pointing at the legitimate project + binary fetched from a publisher-mismatched personal GitHub account + mutable `latest` URL with no integrity check + intentional TLS disable for the child process is an unambiguous arbitrary-code-delivery channel against installers running the CLI.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
